analysis_description: Round 3 tapes days 0–2: ATM (nearest strike) BS implied vol from voucher mid vs short-horizon realized vol of VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT; model IV from pooled quadratic smile; mean VEV bid-ask spread from top-of-book. Confirms IV≈model on average (mean iv_minus_model≈0) while mean iv_minus_rv is negative on subsample—co-movement with book width is weak (corr≈-0.06).
used_to_decide: Ground joint VEV width on iv_atm − rv_ann with T from round3description + intraday wind-down (plot_iv_smile_round3). After v0/v1 backtests, hydrogel day-2 losses dominated; v2 cuts hydrogel size/spread sensitivity while keeping the RV-vs-IV VEV thesis.

analysis_description: Vega-weighted IV regime pass across all 10 VEV strikes on days 0–2 (subsample 200 timestamps): compute BS implied vol per strike from mid, compute vega per strike, then vega-weighted IV minus extract RV proxy. This keeps the strategy anchored to IV/Greeks rather than pure price heuristics.
used_to_decide: vega-weighted IV minus RV remains systematically negative on this tape (mean about -0.093), so simple directional short-vol is weak; retained core joint-width thesis but improved execution by taking only local book mispricings around the same smile-theo and keeping shared regime width logic.

analysis_description: IV smile shape vs microstructure (days 0–2, subsample 200): per timestamp, fit IV (BS from mid) linearly in m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T); report residual skew (IV at max m_t minus at min m_t) and correlate with top-of-book spread on VEV_5200. Confirms no stable linear link between that skew proxy and local spread in this sample.
used_to_decide: Kept the joint RV-vs-ATM-IV width thesis; did not add skew-spread gating. Tried per-tick BS-delta extract hedge (Greek) in code but backtests (v6/v7) trailed v4—disabled by default. Parameter sweep on v4 take edge (v8–v11) nudged PnL; v10 (TAKE_EDGE_MULT=0.63) best in session.

analysis_description: BS theta on focal ATM (VEV_5200): θ = -S·φ(d₁)·σ/(2√T) with r=0, IV from BS inversion of mid at K=5200; carry proxy = max(0,−θ)/S; compared to IV_atm - RV of extract (rolling 30 timesteps) on days 0–2 subsample. Correlation(IV−RV, carry) ≈ -0.11 (weak/negative, not a clean second factor in linear sense).
used_to_decide: Added optional small blend of (carry/S)-scaled time decay into the IV−RV regime in trader_v0; children v12–v15 nest from v10. Micro-sweep: v14 (THETA_REGIME_WEIGHT=0.16) best at 59,976; stronger theta (v13) not better than v12–v14. Still no pivot away from core thesis; benchmark ~800k remains distant.

analysis_description: Vega-weighted mispricing vs book: at each subsample timestamp, for each strike compute model-IV BS theo and vega; mispricing = mid−theo; aggregate vega-weighted mean mispricing and mean top-of-book spread across strikes. Corr(vega_w_mispr, mean_spread) small positive (~0.08) on 150 points—suggests weak co-movement, not a strong linear signal for a second gate in this cut.
used_to_decide: Nested on best parent v14: optional VEV_HALF_MAX clamp in trader_v0; sweeps v16–v21 (cap 32/25, TAKE 0.60/0.615/0.66, theta 0.18) did not improve over v14 (59,976) on R3 0–2. Tie-keep v14 as champion; no thesis change.

analysis_description: Predictive check for core regime: ATM (nearest strike) IV_minus_RV vs next subsample ATM voucher return. Correlation is mildly negative (~-0.126), consistent with weak short-horizon mean reversion in option returns when IV-RV is high. This supports testing small directional center shifts while keeping the same width-thesis framework.
used_to_decide: Added optional REGIME_CENTER_SHIFT in base trader and tested v22-v24; directional shift did not beat v14. Follow-on width sweep (v25-v28) found better extract-side settings while preserving the same IV-vs-RV voucher thesis. Best now v28=60,126.

analysis_description: ATM call gamma from BS (IV = BS-1 of nearest-strike mid, same T and RV as prior cuts) vs IV−RV. Corr(IV−RV, \u03b3_atm) is mildly negative (~-0.08) on 150 subsample points—suggests convexity and IV-richness are weakly inversely co-linear in this crude linear sense.
used_to_decide: Implemented optional GAMMA_REGIME_WEIGHT in trader_v0: scale the same (theta-adjusted) IV−RV joint regime by normalized BS gamma at the ATM (nearest) strike, using IV from inversion. v29 (mild weight) best at 60,493 vs v28 60,126; heavier gamma (v30) regressed. Take-edge nudges v31-v32 did not beat v28.

analysis_description: Contract-by-contract propagation study using next-tick do vs du: high-delta strikes (4000-5200) show much larger betas/correlation to underlying returns than far OTM (6000/6500 near zero), with mixed but observable spread-widening effects after top-decile |du| shocks. Added BS delta reference from inverted IV per strike.
used_to_decide: Implemented strike-aware voucher size/take maps in strategy children (v33/v34) while preserving same IV-vs-RV joint width thesis. Emphasizing high-response strikes and de-emphasizing inert far OTM contracts improved to new best v33=60,627. Also started logging per-run outputs under strategy folder and storing trade/fill summaries in results.json for new runs.

analysis_description: Follow-up on propagation-driven strike maps: high-response ITM strikes dominate do~du beta and notional contribution; far OTM contracts (6000/6500) show near-zero beta and low informative response. Tested whether further reducing far-OTM participation while slightly concentrating around 5200 improves outcome.
used_to_decide: v35 (tighter far-OTM de-emphasis + 5200 bump) tied v33 at 60,627; v36 (simplified conservative bucketing) regressed. Kept strike-aware map family as best current branch, with v33/v35 co-best and v33 preferred as simpler baseline map.

analysis_description: Lead/lag and shock spread: per day, corr(du, do) at lags 0-3 averaged across days 0-2. For focal VEV_5200/5000, contemporaneous correlation du↔do is large (~0.72-0.75) while lag-1 is ~0 (no simple lead). Next-tick spread change after large |du| (top 10% within day): VEV_5200 shows +0.011 mean Δspread on shock vs calm; VEV_5000 slightly negative—so joint shock widening is most consistent with near-ATM liquidity response in 5200.
used_to_decide: Implemented optional SHOCK_VEV_HALF_ADD on |d log S| between extract mids in trader_v0 (same IV−RV joint width thesis). Tested v37-v39 from v33; aggregate PnL unchanged at 60,627 under worse matching—keep hook for future tapes but prefer v33 as simpler unless re-tuned with per-strike shock factors.

analysis_description: Up/down asymmetry extension: computed beta_up vs beta_dn and spread-response asymmetry by voucher. Lower strikes (4000–5100) show largest positive up-vs-down spread widening asymmetry, consistent with directional stress passing through high-delta contracts. Added per-strike shock widening maps focused on those strikes.
used_to_decide: Implemented SHOCK_VEV_HALF_ADD_MAP in base and tested v40-v41 to target low-strike widening under extract shocks. Aggregate PnL tied v33 at 60,627 (no incremental lift under worse fills), so retained v33 as simpler champion tie-break.

analysis_description: Re-used propagation_asymmetry_by_voucher: lower strikes (4000-5100) have largest (mean Δspread|up-shock) minus (mean|down-shock) while 5200-5500 show a different split; signed extract moves let us add half-width on the relevant side only. TRADE_HYDROGEL=False benchmark: activitiesLog per-product pnl at last timestamp (max day, max ts) sums to 49,755 vs 60,627 with active hydrogel—hydrogel adds net +~10,872 in this v33 clone, far from a standalone extract+VEV ~800k bar (likely different aggressor/sizing; our stack remains conservative).
used_to_decide: Added optional UP_SHOCK_VEV_HALF_ADD_MAP and DN_SHOCK_VEV_HALF_ADD_MAP in trader_v0 (when both set, they replace the unsigned per-strike map for the shock component) and v42 to widen low strikes on d log S>0 and 5200-5500 on d log S<0. Backtest still 60,627 (tied v33). v43 disables new hydrogel quotes; logged benchmark PnL and noted hydrogel carry in tradeHistory for context.

analysis_description: Greek + microstructure cross-section on Round 3 tapes (days 0-2, subsample every 50 timestamps): at each tick compute BS vega at model_iv per VEV and top-of-book spread; corr(vega, spread) across strikes averages about -0.45 (600 ticks). Pooled mean spread ~5.6; 4000/4500 are ~+15/+10 vs pool, OTM strikes negative vs pool. Same T as trader (DTE from csv day 8 minus intraday per round3description).
used_to_decide: Added optional VEV_HALF_LOCAL_ADD_MAP + scale in trader_v0 to tilt half-spreads by strike-level spread structure vs pool. v44 used signed mean(spread)-pool offsets (scale 0.07) -> 58,781 worse. v45 used positive offsets only (scale 0.09) -> 56,912 worse. Falsified adding static vega-spread cross-section tilts on top of joint IV-RV width for this backtest; keep hook off in champion v33.

analysis_description: Read round3work/vouchers_final_strategy/STRATEGY.txt and outputs/r3_tight_spread_summary.txt: joint gate (VEV_5200 and VEV_5300 BBO spread <= 2 same timestamp) matches tape analysis (day 0 Welch t on forward extract mid K=20 bars). Merged main added Prosperity Round 3 historical day 3 CSV; backtests now run days 0-3 unless restricted to 0-2.
used_to_decide: Implemented USE_TIGHT_GATE_5200_5300 + bbo_spread() in trader_v0: scales joint IV-RV half_vev, extract/VEV sizes, and extract half_u by tight vs loose regime. TRADE_HYDROGEL=False for v46-v48 (PnL objective = extract + VEV only). v46-v48 with several tight/loose multipliers all trail v33-on-4-days (59,288 worse) and no-hydro v43 (48,055 worse); best gate attempt v47 at 47,710 worse / 44,395 all. Gate not adopted as primary; hooks remain for further tuning. Both match modes logged in results.json per run.

analysis_description: Authoritative pivot to round3work/vouchers_final_strategy only (per new scope). Reread STRATEGY.txt and ORIGINAL_DISCORD_QUOTES.txt (Sonic: both 5200 and 5300 spread at or below 2; t-stat decays when either widens; inclineGod: spread correlation, not price only). Existing 6-panel outputs and r3_tight_spread_summary.txt remain the quantitative reference (day-0 forward mid K=20).
used_to_decide: Shipped trader_v49 (gate-only mid MM, no smile) and trader_v50 (gate + BS model_iv fair from trader_v0 math for execution; logs iv_atm_nearest_strike for Greek documentation; regime is joint spread gate only, not IV-RV). Both TRADE_HYDROGEL omitted. Round 3 days 0-3: v49 worse 10,740 / all 12,910; v50 worse 41,285 / all 42,693. Far below 200k/day floor; next work is larger exploration (sizes, K-step directional extract hint, or restrict days) under the same folder thesis.

analysis_description: Reread updated STRATEGY.txt (Sonic: ~2x per-event edge when both legs tight; layer-2 optional long extract on forward-mid read). v51 adds: larger tight-regime clips (inclineGod path via one-tick d(mid)); spread–spread product still in traderData; extract bid-only in tight (long exposure), symmetric in wide. Same BS anchor as v50.
used_to_decide: v51 improves v50 on 4 R3 days: worse 50,206 vs 41,285; all 55,611 vs 42,693. Still not 200k/day; long-only in tight is aggressive (inventory/unwind left to VEVs + wide regime).
